mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when PAYEMS increases by >100k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger corporate earnings momentum, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when INDPRO rises by >1%, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong demand pressures that drive equity market optimism, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL rise, or the interaction term between GDP and inflation loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) reduces liquidity in financial markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the predictive power of FEDFUNDS drops below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when UNRATE declines, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and residential sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated demand for housing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when both HOUST and RSAFS exceed thresholds, or the interaction term between housing metrics loses significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during early-cycle expansion predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when GDPC1 grows, or the predictive power of GDP growth drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.
